$comment: Declarative source of truth for the PingOne entities oauthPlayground needs. A bootstrap script provisions exactly this against a user's environment (idempotent, lookup-by-name). Client IDs/secrets are NOT stored here — they are written to .env after creation. Mirrors the pattern of AI-Demo's scope-topology.json + pingoneProvisionService.js.

resources.name: OAuth Playground API
resources.description: Demo resource server so the playground can show scoped access tokens, introspection, and token exchange against a real audience.

apps.name: OAuth Playground — User App
apps.description: User-facing confidential web app exercising the interactive grants (authorization code + PKCE, refresh, device, CIBA, token exchange).

apps.name: OAuth Playground — SPA (public, PKCE no secret)
apps.description: Optional public single-page-app client for demonstrating PKCE without a client secret (token_endpoint_auth_method NONE). Same callbacks as the user app.

apps.name: OAuth Playground — Worker App
apps.description: Worker app for the Client Credentials / Worker-Token flow AND the bootstrap's own Management-API calls. Uses client_secret_basic. Authorizes via assigned ROLES, not scope grants.

notes: redirectUris are the cross product of hosts × redirectUriPaths (localhost:3000 + api.ping.demo:3000).
PingOne may reject some grant combos on a single app; the bootstrap should create per the manifest and report per-app which grants applied, splitting into extra apps only if validation fails.
Implicit / Hybrid / ROPC are deliberately omitted from the default user app (deprecated in OAuth 2.1). Add a separate 'legacy' app entry if those flows must run against real PingOne.
WORKER apps cannot hold scope grants — they authorize via assigned admin roles (set in the PingOne console or via role-assignment API).
